skill.name: ckm-slides
skill.description: Create strategic HTML presentations with Chart.js, design tokens, responsive layouts, copywriting formulas, and contextual slide strategies.
skill.summary: Create persuasive HTML slide decks with Chart.js charts, design tokens, responsive layouts, and copywriting formulas.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: The skill is documentation for generating HTML presentations, not an executable package. Static external command, filesystem, sensitive file, and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown fences, CSS terms, and presentation text. The only confirmed risk is low-severity external resource loading for Chart.js and optional background images.

low_findings.title: External CDN Dependency in Generated Deck Template
low_findings.description: The HTML template loads Chart.js from cdn.jsdelivr.net. This is a legitimate CDN dependency, but generated decks will make a network request and inherit normal third-party script supply-chain risk. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The URL is directly visible in the template, and there is no evidence of credential collection or suspicious exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly visible in the template, and there is no evidence of credential collection or suspicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Optional External Background Image Reference
low_findings.description: The background image example references images.pexels.com. This is a normal stock image host, but generated slides may disclose viewer IP metadata to that image provider when loaded. Confidence: 0.92. Confidence reasoning: The external image URL is explicit and limited to a presentation background example, with no hidden data parameters.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external image URL is explicit and limited to a presentation background example, with no hidden data parameters.

content.value_statement: Professional slide decks need strong structure, clear visuals, and persuasive copy. This skill provides HTML templates, Chart.js examples, layout patterns, and copywriting formulas for faster deck creation.
actual_capabilities: Creates HTML presentation structures with slide navigation and progress indicators
Uses responsive 16:9 slide layouts with mobile and tablet breakpoints
Adds Chart.js examples for line, bar, doughnut, and radar charts
Applies design tokens through CSS custom properties for colors and typography
Maps slide goals to copywriting formulas such as PAS, AIDA, and FAB
Recommends deck structures for pitches, sales decks, demos, and internal updates
limitations: Generated decks are HTML files and are not native PowerPoint or Keynote files
The default Chart.js template loads the library from a public CDN
The skill provides reference patterns, not a full presentation editor
Users must supply accurate business data, brand details, and final copy approvals

use_cases.title: Investor Pitch Deck
use_cases.description: Build a structured seed or Series A deck with problem, solution, traction, market, team, and ask slides.

use_cases.title: Sales Presentation
use_cases.description: Create a persuasive sales deck with pain points, proof, pricing, objection handling, and a clear call to action.

use_cases.title: Executive Update
use_cases.description: Prepare a QBR, board update, or all-hands presentation with metrics, charts, timelines, and concise narrative flow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Single Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one HTML title slide for [topic]. Use a strong hook, short subtitle, and responsive layout.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Data Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a metrics dashboard slide for [business area]. Include three key metrics and one Chart.js chart using this data: [data].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Persuasive Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-slide problem-solution deck for [audience]. Use PAS for the problem, FAB for the solution, and a clear CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Full Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete [slide count]-slide HTML deck for [goal]. Use the best matching deck strategy, layout patterns, and copy formulas.

output_examples.input: Create a title slide for a fintech startup that speeds up cross-border payments.
output_examples.output: A title slide with a direct headline, short supporting line, centered layout, brand color tokens, and a fade-up entrance.

output_examples.input: Create a traction slide showing monthly revenue growth from 5K to 45K over four months.
output_examples.output: A metrics slide with a line chart, four month labels, revenue values, a concise takeaway, and accessible chart colors.

output_examples.input: Create a sales problem slide about slow manual reporting.
output_examples.output: A problem slide using PAS copy, a strong pain statement, one consequence metric, and a visual layout for quick scanning.

best_practices: Provide audience, goal, slide count, and source data before asking for a full deck
Review all generated business claims and financial figures before presenting
Replace public image URLs and CDN links when offline or private viewing is required
anti_patterns: Do not paste confidential customer data into prompts unless your environment allows it
Do not ask for many unrelated slide goals in one deck without a clear narrative
Do not rely on generated copy for legal, financial, or compliance claims without review

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create HTML slide decks with responsive layouts, navigation, charts, and persuasive copy structure.

faq.question: Does it support charts?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference template includes Chart.js examples for common presentation charts.

faq.question: Can it create PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is focused on HTML presentations, not native PowerPoint or Keynote output.

faq.question: Is internet access required?
faq.answer: The default template loads Chart.js from a CDN, so internet access is needed unless you replace that dependency.

faq.question: What deck types are covered?
faq.answer: It covers pitch decks, sales decks, product demos, QBRs, board updates, webinars, and workshops.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
